content.text: have you been wanting to start your
small business for some time now but
you're just not quite sure where to even
begin and do you want the luxury of
waking up and getting to work right from
the comfort of your own home plus
creating your schedule however you want
it if you're new here my name is noelli
and I am the owner of my small business
noelli creates and a second small
business called Puppy Lu I started my
first business noelli creates almost 2
years ago and since then grown into a
bigger scale where I can run it
full-time right from home in this video
I'll be sharing how to turn a thought
into action and the steps you'll need to
take to start a small business in
general but also how to run that
business from home if you're a returning
subscriber you'll know how long awaited
this video has been and if you're new
here make sure to subscribe to see all
things small business on this channel
including behind the scenes DIYs hacks
tutorials small business budgeting tips
for saving money and so much more I also
just want to say that I won't be
covering any legal things in this video
just because the laws in every state and
country are different so I won't be
touching too much on that but anyways
let's just jump right into it first
we're going to go over some preparations
and some things that you really need to
think about if you truly want to start
this business the one thing that all
businesses have in common is they all
stem from the same place just an idea if
you already know what you want to do or
what you want to sell that's great I do
however have some advice that I'd want
to hear if I was starting from scratch
there is nothing wrong with wanting
Financial Freedom or to be rich money
solves a lot of problems and allows you
to live comfortably however passion and
happiness is just as if not more
important than money and the best
combination is choosing something that
makes you happy and that you're
passionate about and finding a way to
make a business out of doing something
you love anyways that's just my thoughts
on it and now back to the idea here's a
couple questions to consider obviously
what do you want to do or what do you
want to sell can you run the business
from home do you have a space or can you
make the space to run the business from
home does a require shipping how far is
the nearest post office how much do you
have to invest have you saved up or do
you have the funds do you have a safety
net or a foundation are you stable
enough to start this business physically
mentally and financially I know
sometimes emotions can take over and you
get too excited but do not make any
impulsive decisions and be responsible
do not blow all of your money and forget
your financial obligations and can you
make the time to be consistent this is
the key to growth so if you have to are
you willing to work all weekend to get
it done these are literally the bare
minimum of things to consider and I
would make sure these check out before
making any decisions I don't want to set
anyone up for failure so you have to
know that consistency and sustainability
are key moving on to the research phase
it's now time to get informed and make a
business plan and I want to let you know
what you're getting into so let me just
say this now and warn you for what's to
come hours and hours and hours and hours
and hours of research and I don't say
that lightly this is all going to depend
on the type of business that you want to
do for example the research for how to
start selling digital files online is
going to look very different from
something like how to start selling cups
online regardless here are some things
that you should be researching and
questioning will you be making your
product if not where will you get it
manufactured and how much does that cost
if yes how do you make it what does it
take what materials or supplies you need
and how much is that going to cost where
do you find the supplies where are you
going to sell that product are you going
to make your own website through
something like Shopify or you going to
do Etsy Facebook Marketplace and how
much does that cost if you make your own
website you will be responsible for the
traffic to that website are you okay
with that What Makes You Different why
will someone choose to buy from you a
lot of the times these things are
overlooked and it's so important to
consider literally anything and
everything no matter how small or
insignificant it may seem you want to be
informed as much as possible to make
sure you have a much easier experience
mind you again these are the bare
minimum questions and I highly encourage
you to make a full plan on execution to
see the bigger picture and strategize
once you pass the research phase and
have more insights on what it's going to
take now it's time for the setup you'll
first want to come up with a business
name and logo something I wish I did
differently was not including my name in
there I wish I had picked something more
generalized like I did for my second
business puppy Lux it's hard to stick
out of the crowd with a name like noell
creates because anyone else can do the
same thing and you always want to stand
out in the crowd with poppy it's more
unique and authentic but still
generalizes that the business centers
around dogs this is just my take on it
and something I wish I would have done
differently but of course you're free to
choose whatever you'd like a key point
I'd like to make about choosing a name
is that it should be consistent on all
social medias your website and your
email this is is super important for
many things but mostly for the ease of
your customers and Prospects to find you
anywhere prior to choosing a name make
sure that the website domain name is
available and you can check that by
heading over to godaddy.com and type in
your desired domain name and if you're
like what the heck what's a domain name
it's literally just the name of your
website for example mine is noell
creates. you'll also want to check to
see if the social media handles are
available the main ones being Instagram
Tik Tok and YouTube so check all those
platforms to ensure that they can all be
cohesive and check to make sure if your
business email is available as well and
to start you can just use Google email
that's what I did once you've confirmed
and claimed a name it's now time to
create a logo and brand identity to be
completely honest your brand identity is
not a top priority at first because in
my experience that kind of comes
together as you grow but if you still
want to establish that I would recommend
it you will most definitely need a logo
for your customers to recognize you some
tips I have for creating a logo are to
choose something professional and in SVG
format I did create my logo on canva
which is an amazing business tool and
free to use however a lot of other
business owners also use canva to create
their logos and that's why you may see a
lot of similarities or repetition
because everyone uses the same templates
however I do believe if you want to
download svgs from canva you have to
have the paid version which is only $10
a month and I definitely recommend it
because canva Pro is Elite you can also
use another tool called inkscape which
is like the free version of Adobe
Photoshop though it is still pretty
complex and takes practice to get the
hang of and this tool is amazing for
graphic designs and so many aspects of
businesses so if you have the time I
would definitely try to learn to use it
and on inkscape you will be able to
create an SVG format logo on there which
is so important for many things down the
line but mostly important for looking
professional and to be original and
unique and authentic and that's because
you can create your logo from scratch
but it is okay to take inspiration from
others we all do it but there is a fine
line between inspiration copying and
borderline impersonation now that that
you've kind of built the foundation it's
time to take action and this could look
like ordering all the supplies that
you'll be needing and start practicing
your craft or if you're going to be
using a machine learn how to do that and
if you have physical products you will
want to get prepared but do not
Overstock or make a bunch of products at
once believe me I made this mistake when
I first started I got too excited and
started making a bunch of different
things and I had stuff sitting on the
shelf for months and months for example
if you're doing a t-shirt business I
recommend only ordering shirts in your
size at first to practice on or to use
for your product pictures and your
marketing your videos because you don't
want them to go to waste if they end up
not selling side note if you want to
start a t-shirt or sweat your business
but you're on a budget I can show you
how to start that business for under
$500 leave a comment down below if you
want to see that video you'll also want
to start setting up your social medias
and start posting show the setup process
show the behind the scenes countdown to
the launch date start finding your
customers and building your community
and if you don't know what to post look
at others for inspiration but always add
your own unique twist to it you you'll
also want to start setting up your shop
or your website wherever you're going to
be selling on here are some things to
think about and consider do you want to
ship only in your country or will you do
worldwide shipping and what's that going
to take and how will you do it you also
want to come up with some store policies
like shipping and refunds and will you
be accepting returns how much will you
charge for shipping and will that be
included in the price of your product
will you offer free shipping these are
all things to think about you're going
to need some products for your website
so you want to start listing those you
need to calculate all your costs and do
not Overlook things here's an example if
you're selling a glass cup that costs
you $10 for the blank and $5 for the
design and you're only charging $20 but
you're not accounting for other things
like your labor or your packaging
materials that aren't fixed into the
shipping fee those margins are going to
be pretty slim and unsustainable granted
that was just an example but again
something to note and consider side note
regardless of where you're going to be
selling whether that's Etsy or your own
website you will need a return address
for shipping packages you can can use
your own address though I don't
recommend that I personally use a PO box
but you can also use a virtual address
you'll also want to customize your page
and make it presentable to your
customers something super important to
remember is that people like easy and
convenient if you make your website hard
to navigate and if the customer has to
hassle or struggle more than likely the
customer won't go through the trouble to
find what they're looking for once you
have everything set up your products are
listed your shipping is good to go now
you're ready to launch your business you
probably won't feel like you're ready
and that's totally normal but you just
have to throw yourself out there and
start it's impossible to have it all
figured out right away I'm almost 2
years into this and I still learn new
things every single day like I don't
know what I'm doing sometimes the most
important thing is to just start and I
promise you you will figure things out
as you go trust the process and keep
showing up your first sale may take a
day a week a month you truly don't know
but I do know that it will happen
eventually if you're putting in the work
that was the simplest rundown of how to
start a business and now I'll be
answering some small business questions
that you guys asked me this is probably
the most asked question that I am
constantly getting and it's where do you
get things manufactured and where do I
find supplies or suppliers and a lot of
people don't realize I have a whole
Amazon favorites page with almost all
the supplies I use and I always have
that Linked In the description all of
the custom packaging that you guys see
in my packing videos that is from
sticker mu and I've been using them
since the start of my business and I
love that they actually have affordable
options and samples with no limits which
is so convenient and budget friendly
when you're first starting out and I
also always have a $10 credit Linked In
the description for you guys to use if
you want to try out sticker mu okay and
as for the other supplies that I don't
get on Amazon or sticker mu I find all
my supplies by researching on Google you
will find everything you need on there
if you're willing to do the work it
takes a lot of time money and trial and
error to find good reliable suppliers
something to remember when you're
searching for suppliers is that
affordable or cheap doesn't always equal
good quality and if you want to direct
answers or references for suppliers you
probably won't find them without paying
for it again a lot of time money and
trial and error goes into it and
businesses aren't willing or obligated
to share that information mind you all
that information is available for free
online you just have to search for it so
don't waste any time waiting around for
answers go out there and search for them
yourself all right I touched on this a
little bit early in the video but I'll
elaborate a little bit more this is
really going to depend on the type of
business that you're doing but I still
don't recommend overstocking at first
more particularly if your product
involves different variations like sizes
or colors you don't want to have 20 size
small shirts at first because later down
the line you might notice that size
large are your best sellers maybe you
order a bunch of products in the color
red when only the color pink sells the
best later on believe me I made mistakes
like this and it cost me hundreds of
dollars I'm almost 2 years in and I
still have products like screen print
transfers sitting on the shelf that I
cannot get rid of so think of the
longevity and if your product can be
repurposed before stocking too many okay
so I truly can't can't tell you how to
price your merch because again this
depends on so many factors for example
let's say I sell sulated tumblers and
Laser engrav tumblers sublimated
tumblers take more materials but it only
takes less than 2 minutes to press on
the other hand laser engrave tumblers
take less materials but if you're doing
something like a full wrap that's going
to take about 30 to 40 minutes maybe
more depending on the details you have
to take into account the time materials
machinery and still think about paying
yourself a liable wage and when it comes
to shipping costs you have to think
about not only the cost of the label
which is essentially the cost of how to
get the package from point A to B but
also the shipping materials used to
package the order what I will say though
is that the shipping material should not
be coming out of your pocket and should
be included in the price of the product
or in the price of shipping and you can
always price your shipping rates by
weight or the amount of money spent on
an order for example I offer my
customers free shipping on orders over
$100 you definitely need to dis all the
costs and charge accordingly I made the
mistake of not figuring it out until
after a couple months of starting and it
cost me a lot of money there's no exact
amount I can tell you on how much it
cost to start a business but what I will
say is that I made some poor financial
decisions when I first started please
know that there is a huge difference
between investing and spending from the
start I was already full-time so I did
go all in and I was irresponsible with
where my money went and that's why I
mentioned earlier in the video video not
to blow all your money and forget your
financial obligations I used almost all
the money I had so I took out some
credit cards and only when I was down to
my last around
$1,000 that's when I had no choice but
to use whatever I made from the business
and the money from the sales was going
right back into the business I didn't
pocket anything for about 8 months and
that's when I started actually profiting
if I could do it all over again I would
100% focus on my research and
calculating costs before actually
purchasing anything I mentioned that
excitement and emotions can take over
but it's so important to be smart and
make informed decisions because you
don't know how long it's going to take
to start seeing that money come back
being a small business owner will
probably mean that you'll have to become
a content creator the way I grew my
business and God sales was all through
social media and in this day and age
there is no better place to Market your
business for free than on the internet
and know you don't have to spend
hundreds of dollars on ads to Market
your business when you can grow an
audience and customer base organically I
have never used any paid efforts to
promote my business I did however do a
lot of research on growing on social
media and how to reach my target
audience and most importantly how to
build a community sales are great don't
get me wrong but often times the people
who are engaging in interacting with
your content are just supporters who
like you and your content be consistent
and keep showing up that's the only way
your business will grow and focus on
strategy and post with purpose okay now
that I've kind of answered all the group
questions I'm going to be answering some
miscellaneous questions so here we go
how do I deal with bad reviews or
customers here's the thing if you make a
mistake always take accountability and
make it right but unfortunately even if
you do everything right you will always
have experiences with unpleasant
customers you have to go into this
knowing that you can't please or make
everyone happy and not everyone is going
to love what you have to offer however
that is not grounds for customers to
insult or verba abuse you being your own
boss means that you have have the right
to refuse service and not have to take
insults or deal with rude customers it's
2024 now and I think we can all agree
that the customer is not always right
have you ever heard that saying that
goes never mix business with pleasure or
a friendship founded on business is
better than a business founded on
friendship I don't want to be the one to
crush anyone's dreams but I also want to
be as honest as possible I will speak on
my personal experience as a solo
business owner and let me tell you I
cannot imagine having to run all my
Decisions by someone else that's just
how I feel about it and I would
thoroughly think it through as a matter
of fact I wouldn't even tell anyone if I
was starting a business no friends or
family unless they absolutely have to
know for example those in your household
who will inevitably find out keep it
very quiet and work in silence and I
know from personal experience but
unfortunately often times your friends
and family are your biggest haters and
keep malicious eyes on you and if anyone
does find out which was the case for me
don't let anyone get in the way of
chasing your dreams misery loves company
and if someone is hating on you it's
because deep down they actually hate
themselves their discouragement and
criticism is a reflection of them not
you I would not be where I am today if I
let someone else's insecurities that
they projected onto me stop me from
chasing my dreams something that eases
my mind when sales are slow is knowing
that it won't get better if I give up
it's not always going to be busy and you
will experience slower periods you have
to push through and remember why you
started I know it can be tough
especially if you rely on your business
to pay the bills having other sources of
income for cash flow is so important in
case you do need the extra help during
those slow periods don't put all your
eggs in one basket and consider looking
for other streams of income the way I
find inspiration for my designs is I'll
usually scroll on Pinterest or head over
to Etsy or even look to other small
businesses and whenever I find something
I like I'll save it to look back on when
I'm creating my designs I always add my
own Creative Touch style I usually now
only handdraw my designs on procreate
but I didn't start off that way at first
I used canva to create designs and this
is such a great starting point for
anyone looking to create their own
designs you are a little limited with
how unique you can get with the designs
on canva because you're basically just
combining a bunch of elements together
so if you want something more detailed
and intricate I would definitely start
learning how to draw on procreate I get
asked a lot about how I make certain
things or how I draw on procreate and I
do have videos on my channel for that
but let me tell you YouTube will
literally have all the answers you need
I know how to embroider work a heat
press I do DTF printing I do silhouette
work I use lasers I know graphic design
and so many other things and I am 100%
self-taught everything I've learned was
because of YouTube except sewing my mom
taught me that so shout out to my mom I
love you but anyways guys yeah you don't
need to pay anyone if you don't want to
or take classes anywhere all you need is
the internet all right guys I think that
was pretty much all the questions and I
hope I covered the majority of them but
if there's anything I missed leave them
in the comments of this video and I will
try to answer them for you I really hope
this video gave you the reassurance you
need to start that business take the
risk and do it without caring what
anyone else has to say or think it's a
lonely road to success but you got to do
what's right for you think about your
future and what you see yourself doing
for the rest of your life just a few
years ago I was working two jobs that
made me miserable and drove me into a
depression de my life had no purpose or
meaning and it felt like every day was a
NeverEnding cycle of sadness fast
forward to now I am the happiest I've
ever been and I'm able to work from home
living off of multiple streams of income
by doing something I love and it was all
because I took the risk and rolled the
dice and you can do it too please let me
know if this video was helpful and if
